headline: T-Mobile US Joins Rivals On Satellite Push To Extend Coverage Story
summary: T-Mobile US, AT&T, and Verizon have agreed to form a joint venture focused on satellite based direct to device connectivity. The initiative is aimed at reducing wireless dead zones across the United States, especially in remote and underserved regions. The collaboration centers on pooling spectrum resources and creating a unified approach to satellite supported coverage. T-Mobile US, listed as NasdaqGS:TMUS, is entering this joint venture at a time when its stock trades at $185.22. The...
